This is complete nonsense.

AI isn’t writing most of the code. This is something only non-coders believe.

If AI were writing most of the code, why would it matter that senior engineers have their heads in the sand? (which they don’t)

Yes senior+ engineers are using LLMs a ton. I do believe that the LLMs of today are better than they were 3 months ago.

AI is absolutely writing most of the code at many companies

Including at ours.

We had a team of 50 engineers at Lambda School building largely the same software we rebuilt at Gauntlet AI.

The software we write today (entirely with AI) with 1.5 engineers is better, more robust, more performant, more scalable, and does more.

It’s here.

Also we ripped out Salesforce and replaced it with AI-generated code in a few weeks and saved over $100k/yr in SaaS spend.

Same with Canvas.
(Obviously we didn’t rebuild all of Salesforce, just the 1% we needed.)

Reddit is blowing up over the story of an obese sphynx cat named Pound Cake. 

He was adopted by an owner who posted weekly about his weight loss journey - and thousands of fans were devastated when he suddenly passed away. 

Until someone discovered he was AI-generated. https://t.co/XMB0tpcJFP

Interestingly - when the first videos of Pound Cake were posted, people on the subreddit reported it as AI media.

1. The Secret to Co-CEOship

Find the sweet spot of shared responsibilities: Scott and I overlap by 60-80%.

We interchange roles & functions over various periods of time.

You need a clear understanding of roles but also a shared context to make it work.

What is your single

2. We Will Have More Devs and More Software in a World of AI

Software will be cheaper to build & we are not running out of ideas for software anytime soon.

There will be more devs & they will be far more efficient.

We will create far more technology.

Will we have more or less https://t.co/Jw1xBdEziG

3. Why None of the AI Startups Today Have a Moat and Why It Does Not Matter

Everyone is copying each other & the switching costs are near zero.

Defensibility in the future will come from value, data, and workflows.

Incumbents & startups both have a good shot at winning.

How

4. Does per Seat Pricing Die in a World of AI

There will be 2 logical replacements: per-value delivered & consumption model.

Customers hate the consumption model & it is hard to measure per-value outcomes.

Seat-based pricing won’t die, but it will likely mix with one of these

5. The Single Greatest Threat to Atlassian Today?

Our biggest threat is losing our creativity.

We want to be a multi-decade technology company.

We need to be constantly reinventing ourselves, not defending past wins.
